figures.caption: Figure 4: Mean hallucination rate (FPR) across config A/B/C. GPT-4o is most precise (0.193); Llama fabricates at 0.417.
Table 10: Summary of benchmark properties and research question answers.
Table 9: Composite score by difficulty and config for three representative models. Collapse: >50% drop from config A to config B.
Figure 3: Difficulty × config heatmap for three models. The Type2 collapse at config B is visible across all three.
Table 1: Comparison of SWE-PRBench with related code review evaluation resources.
Figure 1: SWE-PRBench leaderboard with 95% bootstrap CIs. Top four models cluster (s̄ 0.147– 0.153); clear tier gap at rank 4/5.
Figure 2: Composite score by model and context configuration. All 8 models degrade A→C; the A→B drop is the largest step.
Table 4: SWE-PRBench dataset statistics (350 PRs from 65 of 100 RQS-qualified repositories).
Table 8: SWE-PRBench leaderboard: 100-PR sample, 8 models, sorted by mean difficulty-weighted composite s̄ across all configurations. Parse failures in footnotes. Best per column in bold.

Table 6: The three frozen context configurations. Budgets reflect actual measured token counts from the pipeline; the three configs differ in layer composition, not in token volume (range: 2,000–2,500 tokens).
